external.description: Phrases like ‘climate crisis,’ ‘climate emergency’ or ‘climate justice’ might seem to escalate the urgency, but a large survey shows they don’t help and may actually hurt.
external.title: If you want Americans to pay attention to climate change, just call it climate change

record.text:
A recent survey of 5,137 Americans reveals that familiar terms like "climate change" and "global warming" are more effective in raising concern and prompting action than newer phrases such as "climate crisis" or "climate justice".
#climatechange #climate

theconversation.com/if-you-want-...

external.title: If you want Americans to pay attention to climate change, just call it climate change
external.description: Phrases like ‘climate crisis,’ ‘climate emergency’ or ‘climate justice’ might seem to escalate the urgency, but a large survey shows they don’t help and may actually hurt.

author.displayName: Richard Harris

images.alt: Screenshot of visualisation from BlueGlobe data analytic platform, here showing local precipitation.
10-year rolling average precipitation for Balquhidder Catchment showing trends of months >1 sigma above IPCC baseline and no months below, showing clear trends in both.
Breakdown of annual months with precipitation at 1…3 sigma above and below IPCC baseline of 1961-1990, along with net sum trend and 10-year rolling average

record.text: BlueGlobe - our computational 'Digital Twin' -  helps to bridge the gap between global climate models and the ability to understand and forecast what is happening locally. Here we demonstrate the changing precipitation for a local catchment in Highland Scotland - blueglo.be #climatechange

images.alt: Screenshot of visualisation from BlueGlobe data analytic platform, here showing local precipitation.
10-year rolling average precipitation for Balquhidder Catchment showing trends of months >1 sigma above IPCC baseline and no months below, showing clear trends in both.

author.displayName: anlomedad

record.text:
Zambia gets warm and dry in El Nino conditions, cool & wet in La Nina. 
Matt England et al 2022 modelled AMOC slowdown and found it leads to quasi-permanent La Nina. Also, a slow AMOC traps heat in the S-Hemisphere. So Zambia gets cool&wet La Nina, plus ~2C from dead AMOC plus °C from ClimateChange.
